article_title: Moonshot AI Open-Sources Kimi K2.6, a Trillion-Parameter Model That Runs 300-Agent Swarms for Hours

content_preview.title: Moonshot AI Open-Sources Kimi K2.6, a Trillion-Parameter Model That Runs 300-Agent Swarms for Hours
content_preview.summary: Moonshot released Kimi K2.6 under a modified MIT license, claiming parity with GPT-5.4 and Claude Opus 4.6 on coding benchmarks while orchestrating agent swarms that run for half a day unattended.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Moonshot AI, the Beijing startup behind the Kimi assistant, has released Kimi K2.6, a trillion-parameter open-weight model aimed squarely at the coding and agentic workloads that define the current frontier race. The release landed on April 20, [according to SiliconANGLE](https://siliconangle.com/2026/04/20/moonshot-ai-releases-kimi-k2-6-model-1t-parameters-attention-optimizations/), and was publicly announced the following day alongside the model weights, [as reported by the South ...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News piece at 658 words (within the 400-1200 range). Clear section hierarchy (Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know) with appropriate hedging throughout. Technical details are precise without becoming jargon-soup.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 4 sources re-fetched via WebFetch and cross-checked against on-disk snapshots in sources/2026-04/moonshot-ai-open-sources-kimi-k26-a-trillion-parameter-model-that-runs-300-agent-swarms-for-hours/. SCMP (source-0): supports the 'on par with or better than...GPT-5.4, Claude Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1 Pro' quote verbatim, the 'independent verification of these claims remains limited' quote verbatim, and the 'On Monday, Alibaba Cloud released its latest model, Qwen3.6-Max-Preview, as a closed' line which anchors Alibaba's launch to April 20 (calendar-confirmed Monday). SiliconANGLE (source-1): supports April 20 release, 1T params / 384 experts / 8 activated / 400M vision encoder, MLA, SwiGLU, Hugging Face availability. VentureBeat (source-2, WebFetch rate-limited 429 on re-check, but on-disk snapshot grepped exhaustively): contains '300 sub-agents', '4,000 coordinated', 'SysY', 'five straight', '140 functional' — all verbatim. The-Decoder (source-3): supports 54.0 HLE with Tools / 58.6 SWE-Bench Pro / 83.2 BrowseComp, 'falls behind on pure reasoning and vision', 300 sub-agents at 4,000 steps, >12 hours in Rust/Go/Python, modified MIT with 100M MAU / $20M monthly revenue thresholds.
editor_notes.rewrite_verification: Round 1 REQUEST_CHANGES finding was a fabricated VentureBeat attribution for SWE-Bench Pro competitor scores (57.7 GPT-5.4 / 53.4 Claude Opus 4.6 / 54.2 Gemini 3.1 Pro) plus qualifiers 'high-compute setting', 'maximum effort', 'thinking mode'. Grep of the current body_markdown for all six of those tokens returns ZERO hits — the offending sentence was removed cleanly per recommendation option (a). Grep of the VentureBeat snapshot (source-2.html) confirms zero hits for '57.7', 'high-compute', 'maximum effort', 'thinking mode'; the residual '53.4' and '54.2' matches are SVG path coordinates (M47.8971 14.4816C50.8033... 53.4569... 54.2597...), not prose, consistent with the Round 1 finding. K2.6's own scores (54.0 / 58.6 / 83.2) and all other verified claims are preserved unchanged.
editor_notes.ambiguity_fix: Round 1 secondary recommendation asked for the 'same day' ambiguity between April 20 (SiliconANGLE release) and April 21 (SCMP announcement) to be anchored explicitly. Rewrite now reads 'it arrives on the same April 20 release day that Alibaba pivoted in the opposite direction with the closed-weights Qwen3.6-Max-Preview' — a clean anchor to the SiliconANGLE release day, consistent with SCMP's 'On Monday' reference (April 20, 2026 is a Monday).
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every numeric or quoted claim in the body maps to a cited source. No fabricated attributions remain. No new unsourced claims were introduced in the rewrite.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and appropriately hedged. 'What We Don't Know' section explicitly flags training-compute opacity, first-party-testing provenance of the headline long-horizon runs, and open questions on 300-agent orchestration outside Moonshot's infra.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Moonshot/Kimi coverage in 2026-04 or recent archive.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Round 1 finding fully addressed via clean removal (option a). Secondary date ambiguity also anchored. All four sources re-verified. Ready to publish.
